feed.title: Mend.io Unified Release Notes
feed.subtitle: Aggregated RSS, Atom, and HTML of all Mend release notes.

entry.title: Mend for Bitbucket DC: Version 26.2.1.1 (23-February-2026)
entry.content:
Unified Agent 25.12.2 | Renovate 42.99.0 | Remediate 26.2.1 | Pre-Scan Builder (PSB) 25.8.1

Resolved Issues

  • Fixed an issue where Bitbucket users received a 500 error when creating issues due to invalid severity values with trailing characters. Added validation to ensure only valid severity types are processed, preventing these errors.

  • Fixed an issue where the scanner could get stuck in a loop after a scan timed out, repeatedly logging timeout errors instead of properly cancelling the task. The process now ensures scans are cancelled as expected, improving reliability for long-running scans.

  • Fixed a bug which led to scans running for more than 6 hours and timing out.

  • Fixed an issue where the DockerfileFull output image size exceeded 20GB. The build process was optimized to reduce image size and improve efficiency.

entry.title: Mend for GitLab: Version 26.2.1.1 (23-February-2026)
entry.content:
Unified Agent 25.12.2 | Renovate 42.99.0 | Remediate 26.2.1 | Pre-Scan Builder (PSB) 25.8.1

Resolved Issues

  • Fixed an issue where the scanner could get stuck in a loop after a scan timed out, repeatedly logging timeout errors instead of properly cancelling the task. The process now ensures scans are cancelled as expected, improving reliability for long-running scans.

  • Fixed a bug which led to scans running for more than 6 hours and timing out.

  • Fixed an issue where the DockerfileFull output image size exceeded 20GB. The build process was optimized to reduce image size and improve efficiency.

  • (Remediate) Fixed an issue where the cleanup command did not function correctly when executed in shell mode.

entry.title: Mend for GitHub Enterprise: Version 26.2.1.1 (23-February-2026)
entry.content:
Unified Agent 25.12.2 | Renovate 42.99.0 | Remediate 26.2.1 | Pre-Scan Builder (PSB) 25.8.1

Resolved Issues

  • Fixed an issue where the scanner could get stuck in a loop after a scan timed out, repeatedly logging timeout errors instead of properly cancelling the task. The process now ensures scans are cancelled as expected, improving reliability for long-running scans.

  • Fixed a bug which led to scans running for more than 6 hours and timing out.

  • Fixed an issue where the DockerfileFull output image size exceeded 20GB. The build process was optimized to reduce image size and improve efficiency.

entry.title: Mend for GitHub.com: Version 26.2.2 (08-March-2026)
entry.content:
Resolved Issues

  • (SCA) Fixed an issue where scans of .NET projects would fail with error MSB4057 if a tag existed under in the csproj file. Scans now complete successfully in this scenario.

  • (SCA) Fixed an issue where post-scan cleanup and statistics were not executed if the scan process ended with an error, ensuring proper handling and reporting even when scans fail.

entry.title: Mend Developer Platform: Version 26.2.2 (08-March-2026)
entry.content:
Resolved Issues

  • (SCA) Fixed an issue where scans of .NET projects would fail with error MSB4057 if a tag existed under in the csproj file. Scans now complete successfully in this scenario.

  • (SCA) Fixed an issue where post-scan cleanup and statistics were not executed if the scan process ended with an error, ensuring proper handling and reporting even when scans fail.

entry.title: Mend Unified Agent: Version 26.2.2 (09-March-2026)
entry.content:
Resolved Issues

  • Fixed an issue where the Unified Agent reported incorrect versions for transitive dependencies in Poetry projects using explicit sources, which could trigger false-positive vulnerability alerts. The agent now correctly honors the versions specified in poetry.lock, ensuring accurate dependency reporting.

  • Fixed an issue where source files smaller than 512 bytes, which should be ignored during scanning, were still included in update requests, potentially causing false positive library detections. Now, such files are properly excluded, ensuring more accurate scan results.

entry.title: Mend CLI: Version 26.2.2 (09-March-2026)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • The mend auth command now provides a unified login experience there is no longer a need to select between SAST and SCA during authentication. A single login applies to all scan engines, simplifying CLI setup. Existing environment variable configurations continue to work for backward compatibility.

    • This feature will be rolled out gradually to all environments until March 23rd.

entry.title: Mend AI: Version 25.12.1 (05-January-2026)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • (Premium / Core) Introducing System Prompt Risk (open beta), a new detection and remediation offering by Mend AI, for mitigating risks posed by system prompts used in conversational AI interfaces.

    • A new System Prompt Risk table inventories system prompts and provides quick export/sharing and deep-link side panel for prompt context.

    • System Prompt Risks are integrated into the existing AI Security Risk Factors across Projects and Applications. The classification appears as a Conversational Interface chip, is filterable, and automatically participates in dashboards and workflows.

    • Remediation in the form of a hardened system prompt is available in the System Prompt side panel, providing clear, copy-ready remediation guidance for AppSec engineers and developers.

    • The AI Security Dashboard has been enriched with system prompt risk data.

    • System prompt risk data is also available via API.

entry.title: Mend Container: Version 26.2.2 (08-March-2026)
entry.content:
Resolved Issues

  • Fixed an issue where registry URLs were not being validated correctly, leading to errors during ECR integration.

entry.title: Mend SAST: Version 26.2.2 (09-March-2026)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • Added support for CWE-457: Use of Uninitialized Variable and CWE-824: Access of Uninitialized Pointer to C/C++ analysis in low probability mode.

  • The custom sources and sinks configuration pages now include links to the relevant documentation, making it easier to learn how to define custom taint sources and sinks.

  • When a finding is suppressed and its corresponding GitHub issue is closed, Mend now adds a comment to the issue with the suppression reason, any additional comments, and the user who performed the suppression. This provides a clear audit trail directly within GitHub, regardless of whether the suppression was initiated from a repository-level request or directly on the Mend platform.

  • Suppression request tables now show who approved or rejected each request in a new Reviewed By column, giving security managers a complete audit trail of suppression decisions.

  • The finding details page now includes a refresh button in the Comments tab, allowing you to quickly check for new comments.

Resolved Issues

  • Fixed an issue where adding a custom source in JavaScript did not result in automatically adding the surrounding method as an entry point, potentially preventing the taint source from being reached.

  • Fixed a false positive where Hardcoded Password was incorrectly reported for masked passwords in Python files.

  • Fixed an issue where the Hardcoded Credentials (CWE-798) finding was incorrectly reported as an SQL injection, ensuring accurate vulnerability identification in scan results.

  • Fixed an issue where the Code Compliance report displayed vulnerability counts even after all findings were suppressed, ensuring accurate statistics are shown in compliance and severity reports.

entry.title: Mend SCA: Version 26.2.2 (08-March-2026)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • Added a new EPSS Score column to the Dependencies Findings table, allowing users to view exploit likelihood at a glance and sort by risk. This enhancement streamlines vulnerability triage and prioritization by making EPSS data directly accessible without drilling into details.

  • Added a new dependencyContexts field to the Get Project Security Findings API. This field lets you see how each vulnerable component appears in the dependency graph (direct vs. transitive and its root libraries), enabling clear mapping to the Get Root Libraries Security Findings endpoint and improving reachability analysis.

entry.title: Mend AppSec Platform: Version 26.2.2 (08-March-2026)
entry.content:
New Features and Updates

  • Added a new EPSS Score column to the Dependencies Findings table, allowing users to view exploit likelihood at a glance and sort by risk. This enhancement streamlines vulnerability triage and prioritization by making EPSS data directly accessible without drilling into details.

  • Added a new pre-defined "Auditor" role that provides read-only access for service users requiring data visibility.

    • Auditor role assignments in Legacy SCA will automatically be reflected as such in the Mend AppSec Platform and vice versa.

  • Added support for filtering scans, reports, findings, and violations by the AI scan engine across the Mend AppSec Platform. The AI option is always visible in Scan Engine filters and enabled for customers with relevant entitlements.

  • Added a new dependencyContexts field to the Get Project Security Findings API. This field lets you see how each vulnerable component appears in the dependency graph (direct vs. transitive and its root libraries), enabling clear mapping to the Get Root Libraries Security Findings endpoint and improving reachability analysis.

  • UI Improvements:

    • Updated the table selection experience by moving the select all/deselect all controls into a single checkbox in the table header.

    • Redesigned and relocated the Refresh button for a more intuitive user experience.

    • Updated the Workflows and Reports pages by replacing filters with new filter buttons, making filtering more streamlined and user-friendly.

    • The Reports page now displays the "Delete" bulk action button only when a table row is selected, and the button text has been updated from Remove to Delete for improved clarity.

Resolved Issues

  • Fixed an issue where suppression comments longer than 255 characters were being truncated when retrieved via the UI, API, or CSV export, despite being saved correctly. Now, comments up to 500 characters are fully supported and displayed consistently across all interfaces.
